title: Add basic traffic violation detection with liability percentages - Frontend

requirements.summary: Update AccidentReportForm to accept optional GPS location coordinates and timestamp for enhanced violation detection
acceptanceCriteria: Form includes optional GPS location input fields
Form includes timestamp field (defaults to current time)
GPS and timestamp data are submitted with the accident report

file_operations.description: Add optional GPS location input fields (latitude, longitude) to the form. Add a timestamp field that defaults to the current time but allows manual override. Update the form submission to include gpsLocation as a string (formatted as 'lat,lng' if provided, or empty string if not) in the createReport backend call. Ensure GPS fields are clearly labeled as optional and positioned logically within the existing form layout.

requirements.summary: Create ViolationsDisplay component showing detected violations with type, description, and timestamp
acceptanceCriteria: Component displays violations array from AccidentReport
Each violation shows type, description, and timestamp
Component handles empty violations array gracefully

file_operations.description: Create a new component that accepts a violations prop (array of Violation objects from backend.d.ts). Display each violation in a list format showing violationType, description, and detectedAt timestamp. Format the timestamp as a readable date/time. Use appropriate styling with icons or badges to distinguish violation types (stop sign, U-turn, traffic signal). Handle empty arrays by showing a message like 'No violations detected' with a success/checkmark styling. Use shadcn/ui components (Card, Badge) for consistent styling with the rest of the app.

requirements.summary: Create LiabilityDisplay component visualizing fault percentage split between parties
acceptanceCriteria: Component displays liability percentages for each party
Visual representation clearly shows the percentage split (e.g., 70% / 30%)
Component uses color coding to indicate fault severity

file_operations.description: Create a component that accepts party1Liability and party2Liability props (optional bigint values from AccidentReport). Display the liability split using a horizontal progress bar or stacked bar visualization showing the percentage distribution (e.g., 70% party 1 / 30% party 2). Use color coding: higher fault percentages in more intense colors (red/orange for high liability, green for low). Include numeric percentage labels on or near each segment. Handle cases where liability data is not yet calculated by showing 'Liability analysis pending' or similar message. Use Tailwind CSS for styling and ensure the visualization is responsive.

requirements.summary: Update ReportDetail to display ViolationsDisplay and LiabilityDisplay components
acceptanceCriteria: Both components are positioned logically within the report detail layout

file_operations.description: Import and integrate the ViolationsDisplay and LiabilityDisplay components into the ReportDetail component. Position ViolationsDisplay after the existing DiscrepancyAlert section and before the PhotoGallery. Position LiabilityDisplay near the top of the report detail view, after the fault status badge but before detailed report information. Pass report.violations to ViolationsDisplay and report.party1Liability, report.party2Liability to LiabilityDisplay. Ensure proper spacing and visual hierarchy using existing section styling patterns.
